advancements.husbandry.starcatcher.fishe: Catch a Fish without missing once
emi.starcatcher.entity_entry: This entry will always spawn a %s
emi.info.starcatcher.worms.0: Worms have different fishing modifiers when used as bait.
emi.info.starcatcher.pearls.0: Clams placed on sand will slowly create pearls.
emi.info.starcatcher.pearls.1: Once the clam has opened up you may click on it to harvest a pearl.
emi.info.starcatcher.attachments.0: Hooks, Bobbers & Baits may be attached to your rod using a Tackle Box.
emi.info.starcatcher.attachments.1: They provide several different 'modifiers' which will aid you in different ways, from decreasing lure time, to difficulty reducing buffs.
emi.info.starcatcher.fish_bones.0: Fish Bones are obtained after eating Starcaught Fish.
emi.info.starcatcher.pearl_template.0: This template may be found rarely when collecting pearls from clams.
emi.info.starcatcher.hat.0: Fisherman's hats may be found rarely inside Shipwrecks's chests.
starcatcher.tip.legendary_fish: Most legendary fish love legendary bait!
starcatcher.tip.too_hard: A fish feels too hard? Better rethink which Bobber & Hook you're using!
starcatcher.tip.fisherman_hat: Fisherman Hats can be found rarely inside shipwrecks.
starcatcher.tip.templates: Fishing templates can be used to modify the look of your rod!
starcatcher.tip.tackle_box: Lots of fishes filling up your inventory? Better place them in a tackle box!
starcatcher.tip.secret_messages: Secret messages may sometimes be fished up! Who knows what stories they might tell...
starcatcher.tip.clam: Clams may produce pearls when placed on sand!
starcatcher.tip.aquarium: Too many fish and don't know how to display? Why not build an Aquarium!
starcatcher.tip.weather: Some fish will only come out during specific Weather and Daytime conditions.
starcatcher.tip.golden: Golden Fishes can only be caught once!
starcatcher.tip.wither: Using a wither skeleton skull as a bait might produce... undesirable results...
starcatcher.tip.sell: Fishes will sell for more if they are a low percentile. And even more if they are golden
starcatcher.tip.dimension: You can find fishes everywhere, even in The Nether and The End
starcatcher.tip.ice: Icy fishes will freeze your handle on misses!
tag.starcatcher.placeable_in_tackle_box_: Placeable In Tackle Box Fish Slot
commands.starcatcher.rod_not_found: You must have a StarcatcherCompatible™ rod in your hand
commands.starcatcher.nothing_there: There are no fishes available at the current location. Make sure you're standing inside the correct fluid.
commands.starcatcher.item_empty: You must have an item in your hand to apply the modifier to
starcatcher.configuration.remove_notif_o: Remove New Fish Notif on Hover
starcatcher.configuration.save_data_to_i: Size, Weight, Rarity & Golden NBT saving
starcatcher.configuration.base_chance: Base Chance To Fish Per Tick
gui.starcatcher.processor.fishes_process: - Price is increased by +%s%% based on top 50%%
- Price is multiplied by %sx if golden.
gui.starcatcher.no_hook_or_bobber: A fishing rod won't work without a hook and a bobber...
tooltip.minecraft.fishing_rod.0: A simple fishing rod used for catching
tooltip.minecraft.fishing_rod.2: One can only dream of upgrading
tooltip.starcatcher.starcatcher_guide.0: An angler's best companion, keeping track
tooltip.starcatcher.starcatcher_guide.1: of all the fishes you've caught, and
tooltip.starcatcher.starcatcher_guide.2: those you have yet to reel in!
tooltip.starcatcher.starcatcher_twine.0: A delicate twine wrapped around a stick.
tooltip.starcatcher.starcatcher_twine.1: Vansire used these for string instruments once upon a time.
tooltip.starcatcher.tournament_stand.0: A stand used to host fishing tournaments.
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.prevent_fro: Prevents your handle from freezing up
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.bounce_back: Prevents the fish from swimming away once by bouncing it back up
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.add_creeper: An ill-tempered friend might show up
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.no_gravity: Your bobber will no longer follow the laws of gravity
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.extra_golde: Perfect catches are more likely to become golden
Perfect catches are %s%% more likely to become golden
A golden fish is more likely to bite
A golden fish is %s%% more likely to bite
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.award_treas: Perfect catch may award a treasure
Perfect catches award treasure on a %s%% chance
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.spawn_treas: A treasure spawns after %s consecutive hits
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.hide_catch: You can no longer see your catch whilst fishing
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.freeze_on_m: Missing a sweet-spot will cause your handle to freeze up for a short period
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.spawn_froze: Every so often a frozen sweet-spot might show up
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.spawn_treas: Successfully hitting 3 sweet-spots in a row will summon a treasure chest
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.stop_decay_: Prevents the fish from swimming away for a short period after a hit
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.harder_with: The catch will be harder than usual, but a perfect catch awards a treasure
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.no_flip: Sweet-spots will no longer flip when hit
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.slower_vani: Vanishing sweet-spots will take longer to vanish
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.bigger_gree: Green sweet-spots will be slightly bigger
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.add_leaves_: Leaf sweetspots will appear at random
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.add_aqua_sw: An extra aqua sweet-spot is added
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.add_three_a: Three extra aqua sweet-spot are added
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.flip_every_: Flip every time you hit a sweet-spot
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.adjust_hand: Adjusts the handle's rotation speed by %s%%
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.adjust_vani: Adjusts the vanishing rate of sweet-spots by %s%%
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.adjust_lure: Adjusts minimum ticks to fish by %s%%
Adjusts maximum ticks to fish by %s%%
Adjusts the chance of biting every tick by %s%%
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.adjust_movi: Increases the speed of moving sweet-spots
Decreases the speed of moving sweet-spots
Adjusts the speed of moving sweet-spots by %s%%
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.adjust_deca: Increases the rate at which fish swim away
Decreases the rate at which fish swim away
Adjusts the rate of decay of fishes by %s%%
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.adjust_pena: Increases the penalty for missing sweet-spots
Decreases the penalty for missing sweet-spots
Adjusts the penalty of missing sweet-spots by %s%%
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.skip_miniga: Skips minigame if equipped alongsides the Vanilla-flavoured Bobber
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.luck_attrib: Adjusts fish chance based on your luck
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.burn_on_mis: Missing will cause your handle to lit on fire
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.deep_dark: Hitting sweet-spots will consume you by darkness
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.ignore_dayt: Fishing daytime restrictions will sometimes be bypassed
%s%% chance of ignoring fishing daytime restrictions
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.ignore_weat: Fishing weather restrictions will sometimes be bypassed
%s%% chance of ignoring fishing weather restrictions
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.new_catch_i: Increases the base chance of every uncaught fish by an extra %s
Increases the likelihood of a new catch biting
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.cancel_gold: You can no longer catch golden fishes
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.extra_exp_b: Extra exp is awarded based on your performance
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.force_fish_: If available, will catch the live fish instead
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.guarantee_n: If available, an undiscovered fish will bite
An undiscovered fish is more likely to bite
tooltip.modifier.starcatcher.extra_base_: %s extra fish will come alongside your catch
tooltip.starcatcher.hook.0: A basic iron hook for your fishing rod.
tooltip.starcatcher.bobber.0: A basic bobber for your fishing rod.
tooltips.starcatcher.unknown_fish.0: template fish used in the minigame for entries which hide the item being fished
tooltip.starcatcher.fish_radar.0: A handy radar which displays the fishes
tooltip.starcatcher.fish_radar.1: around the area in a total futuristic way.
tooltip.starcatcher.pearl.0: A beautiful pearl obtained from a clam.
tooltip.starcatcher.fish_bones.1: remains of a once happy fish.
tooltip.starcatcher.treasure.0: A shiny treasure fished by a skilled angler.
tooltip.starcatcher.scalding_treasure.0: A treasure fished directly from the scalding
tooltip.starcatcher.scalding_treasure.1: lands of The Nether. What's inside?

gui.guide.entity: Can be caught alive when using an Almighty Worm as a bait!
gui.guide.always_entity: This fish is more lively than usual!

gui.guide.bait_required: The correct bait is required for this catch!
gui.starcatcher.tournament.ended: %s has ended! The winner is %s with a score of %s!
gui.starcatcher.tournament.ended.no_winn: %s has ended! Nobody caught a single fish :(
gui.starcatcher.tournament.press_tab: Press [Tab] to toggle the tournament's scoreboard.
gui.secret_note.amethyst_hook.2: After yesterday's trouble I decided it's best
gui.secret_note.amethyst_hook.3: I finally get my hands on some diamond tools.
gui.secret_note.amethyst_hook.5: That's what past me thought anyways,
gui.secret_note.amethyst_hook.6: if I knew I would accidentally drop my amethyst
gui.secret_note.amethyst_hook.7: hook down by the deepslate water lakes,
gui.secret_note.amethyst_hook.8: I would have never went down there.
gui.secret_note.amethyst_hook.10: If anyone finds this letter drifting amid the
gui.secret_note.amethyst_hook.11: oceans, you are welcome to take my precious
gui.secret_note.amethyst_hook.12: Amethyst Hook. Once you've caught at least
gui.secret_note.amethyst_hook.14: will shine their blessing upon you and
gui.secret_note.amethyst_hook.15: my amethyst hook will bite your hook.
gui.secret_note.lava_proof_bottle_1.5: I am so tired of these SCAMMERS promising
gui.secret_note.lava_proof_bottle_1.7: survive being submerged in lava! Wanna see
gui.secret_note.lava_proof_bottle_1.8: what I think of your stupid <sclava>Lava-Proof™</sclava>
gui.secret_note.lava_proof_bottle_1.11: This letter is going right into this so-called
gui.secret_note.lava_proof_bottle_1.13: nearest lava pool I can find! Heh that will
gui.secret_note.lava_proof_bottle_2.1: If anyone finds this letter, I would like
gui.secret_note.lava_proof_bottle_2.2: to apologise about my previous letter.
gui.secret_note.lava_proof_bottle_2.6: I must admit after so many scams and
gui.secret_note.lava_proof_bottle_2.8: skeptical and never even gave them a shot.
gui.secret_note.lava_proof_bottle_2.10: What can I say, you live and you learn.
gui.secret_note.lava_proof_bottle_2.12: I have to go now, I heard there's a new
gui.secret_note.hopeful_note.5: It has now been over half a year since I
gui.secret_note.hopeful_note.6: embarked on this journey to find a
gui.secret_note.hopeful_note.7: <scrare>Heart of the Sea</scrare>. My hope grows dim but
gui.secret_note.hopeful_note.8: I will search for it until the end of my days.
gui.secret_note.hopeless_note.5: This is hopeless. There is no <scrare>Heart of the</scrare>
gui.secret_note.hopeless_note.6: <scrare>Sea</scrare>. Those BASTARDS lied to me. I am
gui.secret_note.hopeless_note.7: never returning home. Little Jimmy will forever
gui.secret_note.hopeless_note.8: live with the thought his dad went after an
gui.secret_note.hopeless_note.11: It really is my fault. How could I be such a
gui.secret_note.hopeless_note.12: fool as to believe an item could possibly
gui.secret_note.hopeless_note.13: allow you to breath underwater. Unbelievable.
gui.secret_note.true_blue.9: Ten years or ten moments ago,
gui.secret_note.wither_note.4: I would be more careful if I were you...
gui.secret_note.wither_note.7: You never know what you might catch
tooltip.starcatcher.letter.0: A letter awaiting to be written and sent off.
tooltip.starcatcher.bottled_letter.0: A letter imprisoned in a bottle.
tooltip.starcatcher.bottled_letter.1: The only thing left to do now is throw it into
tooltip.starcatcher.bottled_letter.2: the ocean and let it start its journey.
item.starcatcher.bottled_letter.thrown: Your bottled letter starts its journey...
item.starcatcher.bottled_letter.thrown.f: Your bottled letter seems to have been eaten by a crab... [No Editable Message Data Component found in item, report this to the dev!]
A bottle containing a message written many moons ago.
Looks like the only way to get it out is to break it open.
tooltip.starcatcher.message.0: The message half of a message-in-a-bottle.
tooltip.starcatcher.message.1: Who knows how long it's been drifting the waves to reach you.
tooltip.starcatcher.broken_bottle.0: The remains of an once intact bottle.
tooltip.starcatcher.secret_note.0: An old secret note almost lost to time.
tooltip.starcatcher.secret_note.1: Holding nothing more than the thoughts of
tooltip.starcatcher.secret_note.2: those probably not amongst us anymore.
tooltip.starcatcher.starcaught_fish.0: A starcaught fish, stripped from its uniqueness to fit with the rest of the stack.
tooltip.starcatcher.starcaught_fish.1: A shame it had to end this way really.
tooltip.starcatcher.cooked_starcaught_fi: Nothing is unique about what remains of this once beautiful fish.
tooltip.starcatcher.starcaught_bucket.cr: [CREATIVE MODE] This bucket will always spawn an Aurora. To get the corresponding [Bucket of X] you must catch it through normal means using a bucket as a bait.
If you wish to summon the fish entity, you can right click on the ground with the fish item in creative mode.
tooltip.starcatcher.boot.0: An incredibly high quality boot fished from a lake.
tooltip.starcatcher.driftfin.0: A small fish which spends most of its time in overworld lakes.
tooltip.starcatcher.rainfin.0: A fish common to overworld lakes only seen during rainy days.
tooltip.starcatcher.rainfin.1: Unlike what its name would suggest, the fins are not made of rain.
tooltip.starcatcher.peakdweller.0: A gold colored fish common to mountain lakes.
tooltip.starcatcher.peakdweller.1: It is usually ignored as its hard scales make for tough cuisine use.
tooltip.starcatcher.rockgill.0: A rock-hard fish common to the high mountains.
tooltip.starcatcher.rockgill.1: It is said to make for a great stew,
tooltip.starcatcher.rockgill.2: at least for those who have the teeth to bite through its skin.
tooltip.starcatcher.silverveil_perch.0: A small perch with scales resembling silver.
tooltip.starcatcher.elderscale.0: A deceiving fish often found in lakes.
tooltip.starcatcher.elderscale.1: Elderscales may live up to several decades but often spend their youth hiding.
tooltip.starcatcher.elderscale.=2: Spotting a young one is little more than a rumor, therefore its name.
tooltip.starcatcher.sunny_sturgeon.0: A rare sturgeon often found high up in mountains during sunny days.
tooltip.starcatcher.twilight_koi.0: A rather rare species of Koi said to only come out to feed at night.
tooltip.starcatcher.ripple_catfish.1: drawn to the gentle ripples of falling rain.
tooltip.starcatcher.sun_seeking_carp.1: seen only during daytime poking its head out the water.
tooltip.starcatcher.thunder_bass.0: A rare species of bass drawn to the rumble of thunderstorms.
tooltip.starcatcher.thunder_bass.1: Often seen swimming alongside a Lightning Bass.
tooltip.starcatcher.lightning_bass.0: A rare species of bass drawn to flashes of lightning during thunderstorms.
tooltip.starcatcher.lightning_bass.1: Often seen swimming alongside a Thunder Bass.
tooltip.starcatcher.frostjaw_trout.0: A trout species known for it's razor-sharp
tooltip.starcatcher.crystalback_trout.0: A hardy trout species which uses its
tooltip.starcatcher.crystalback_trout.1: crystal-covered back to defend itself from predators.
tooltip.starcatcher.wintery_pike.1: Often seen stalking prey beneath frozen lakes.
tooltip.starcatcher.blue_ice_pike.0: A predatory fish with scales made of blue ice.
tooltip.starcatcher.blue_ice_pike.1: It vanishing into the frozen waters as soon as it senses danger bigger than itself.
tooltip.starcatcher.aurora.1: It's size makes for an incredibly evasive catch,
tooltip.starcatcher.aurora.2: made even harder by its slippery, crystal-frozen body.
tooltip.starcatcher.sandtail.0: A long sand-covered fish, fond only in warm climates
tooltip.starcatcher.scorchfish.0: A fish species which despises the rain,
tooltip.starcatcher.scorchfish.1: preferring the warmer climate of the desert.
tooltip.starcatcher.mirage_carp.0: A carp species found on hot sunny days in warm climates.
tooltip.starcatcher.mirage_carp.1: Its said desert villagers gave it its name,,
tooltip.starcatcher.mirage_carp.2: as spotting one was often just a mirage.
tooltip.starcatcher.agave_bream.0: A rare bream which emerges only on clear nights
tooltip.starcatcher.agave_bream.1: Its long fins help it navigate the murky waters of desert oases
tooltip.starcatcher.agave_bream.2: It is said to have a fondness for mangosteens
tooltip.starcatcher.cactifish.0: Despite its nam, it is not connected to cacti in any way.
tooltip.starcatcher.cactifish.1: Its green colored scales simply resemble the colors of a cactus.
tooltip.starcatcher.oasis_sturgeon.1: So few have seen it that its existence is not fully confirmed.
tooltip.starcatcher.oasis_sturgeon.2: Legend claims every oasis is guarded by one silently watching beneath the waters.
tooltip.starcatcher.mossy_boot.0: A boot, but this time it's mossy!
tooltip.starcatcher.sludge_catfish.0: A dirty catfish species common in murky swamp waters.
tooltip.starcatcher.sludge_catfish.1: It makes a surprisingly good meal if cleaned properly.
tooltip.starcatcher.lily_snapper.0: A rare, long-bodied snapper found in swamps.
tooltip.starcatcher.lily_snapper.1: It catches smaller fish with its long pointed mouth.
tooltip.starcatcher.sage_catfish.0: A sage-colored catfish found in quiet swamps.
tooltip.starcatcher.sage_catfish.1: It only comes out on clear nights,
tooltip.starcatcher.sage_catfish.2: when the water is calm and still.
tooltip.starcatcher.pale_pinfish.0: A tiny pale pinfish, only found around midnight
tooltip.starcatcher.pale_pinfish.1: in dark forests. It wishes to exist in a version
tooltip.starcatcher.pinfish.0: A tiny pale pinfish, only found around midnight
tooltip.starcatcher.pale_carp.0: A carp species common to the [redacted].
tooltip.starcatcher.pale_carp.1: Their habitat is still under construction in this
tooltip.starcatcher.pale_carp.2: universe so instead they stick to the dark forest.
tooltip.starcatcher.blossomfish.0: A beautiful fish resembling the pink petals of Cherry Grooves.
tooltip.starcatcher.blossomfish.1: It prefers to swim around in the warm sunlight.
tooltip.starcatcher.vesani.0: This species of fish is really good
tooltip.starcatcher.vesani.2: It's unusual to see them out in the
tooltip.starcatcher.petaldrift_carp.0: A pink carp common to Cherry Grooves which
tooltip.starcatcher.petaldrift_carp.1: prefers to come out during the rain.
tooltip.starcatcher.pink_koi.0: A pink koi most commonly found in Cherry Grooves.
tooltip.starcatcher.pink_koi.1: It comes out during rainy days to feed.
tooltip.starcatcher.rose_siamese_fish.0: A rare gorgeous fish found during rainy days.
tooltip.starcatcher.rose_siamese_fish.1: A decently challenging catch for even experienced anglers.
tooltip.starcatcher.livid_bamboo.0: A piece of bamboo, but as a fish. Or so it is said.
tooltip.starcatcher.petal_bass.0: A species of bass adapted to live amongst the flowers of the Overworld.
tooltip.starcatcher.crystalback_sturgeon: A sturgeon often found in high altitudes,
known for it's crystalback which helps it passively
tooltip.starcatcher.icetooth_sturgeon.0: Found high up in the mountains, this sturgeon
tooltip.starcatcher.icetooth_sturgeon.1: uses it's sharp petrified ice teeth to bite down on prey.
tooltip.starcatcher.boreal.0: A <sclegendary>legendary</sclegendary> fish found in icy mountains.
tooltip.starcatcher.boreal.1: A real challenge even for skilled anglers, they are
tooltip.starcatcher.boreal.2: known for blending into the icy waters making it hard
tooltip.starcatcher.crystalback_boreal.0: Although named after the <sclegendary>legendary</sclegendary> fish <sclegendary>Boreal</sclegendary>, this
tooltip.starcatcher.crystalback_boreal.1: fish is not nearly as hard to catch. Its shiny back makes it
tooltip.starcatcher.crystalback_boreal.2: easy to keep track and the crystallized back slows down its swimming speed.
tooltip.starcatcher.silverfin_pike.0: A large pike common to rivers.
tooltip.starcatcher.carpenjoe.0: A majestic carp glowing in modding knowledge.
tooltip.starcatcher.carpenjoe.1: It is said anglers eat it for good luck before debugging.
tooltip.starcatcher.willow_bream.0: A tricky catch only found during nighttime.
tooltip.starcatcher.willow_bream.1: Its blue scales makes it incredibly hard to track whilst reeling it in.
tooltip.starcatcher.drifting_bream.0: A species of bream known for slowly
tooltip.starcatcher.downfall_bream.0: A hard to spot but easy to catch bream,
tooltip.starcatcher.downfall_bream.1: only coming out during rainy nights.
tooltip.starcatcher.hollowbelly_darter.0: A really small easy to catch river fish.
tooltip.starcatcher.mistback_chub.0: A river fish known for its silver colored scaled
tooltip.starcatcher.bluegigi.0: Not to be confused with a bluegill,
tooltip.starcatcher.bluegigi.1: this small fish is rather common around rivers.
tooltip.starcatcher.dried_seaweed.0: A piece of dead seaweed which got
tooltip.starcatcher.crystalback_minnow.0: A really small fish often used as bait.
tooltip.starcatcher.crystalback_minnow.1: This specific species is most common at nighttime.
tooltip.starcatcher.azure_crystalback_mi: A rare mutation of crystalback minnow's
which glows a soft blue tone during midnight.
A challenging catch due to it's tiny size.
tooltip.starcatcher.blue_crystal_fin.0: An uncommon fish most commonly found during daytime.
tooltip.starcatcher.morganite.0: An ocean fish resembling Morganite gemstones.
tooltip.starcatcher.ironjaw_herring.0: This type of herring common to the ocean and often
tooltip.starcatcher.ironjaw_herring.1: has a petrified silvery jaw resembling iron.
tooltip.starcatcher.blue_herring.0: A very illusive fish, always searching for the 46th ocean.
tooltip.starcatcher.blue_herring.1: Fisherman use it for directions as it always goes back to where it all started.
tooltip.starcatcher.deepjaw_herring.0: A fish common to oceans, often regarded highly for trading.
tooltip.starcatcher.dusktail_snapper.0: A heavy fish for it's size often used in dishes.
tooltip.starcatcher.joel.0: A rare <sclegendary>legendary</sclegendary> fish found in oceans.
tooltip.starcatcher.redscaled_tuna.0: A large tuna with red colored scales.
tooltip.starcatcher.redscaled_tuna.1: A single one of these often feeds an entire village.
tooltip.starcatcher.sea_bass.1: No, wait—it's at least a C+!
tooltip.starcatcher.waterlogged_bottle.0: A bottle thrown into the ocean many moons ago.
tooltip.starcatcher.waterlogged_bottle.1: Whatever it once held, it's long gone by now.
tooltip.starcatcher.bigeye_tuna.0: A big heavy fish known for it's big eye.
tooltip.starcatcher.bigeye_tuna.1: This species is only found in deep ocean waters.
tooltip.starcatcher.bigeye_tuna.2: It's said to hang around other Kaupen fish.
tooltip.starcatcher.conch.0: The remains of a mollusk with a spiral shell often used as an ornament.
tooltip.starcatcher.clam.0: Once a bivalve mollusk living in the sandy shores, now just a shell.
tooltip.starcatcher.shroomfish.0: An antique <sclegendary>legendary</sclegendary> fish species taken over by the spores
tooltip.starcatcher.shroomfish.1: of the Mushroom Islands. A really tasty dish for mushroom enjoyers.
tooltip.starcatcher.sporefish.0: A rare fish found in the Mushroom Islands, may prove a
tooltip.starcatcher.gold_fan.0: An underground fish with a tail that resembles a fan.
tooltip.starcatcher.gold_fan.1: As funny as it would be, it does not spin its tail like a fan to move around.
tooltip.starcatcher.geode_eel.1: Their color and texture comes from infused amethyst
tooltip.starcatcher.geode_eel.2: due to their eggs being hatched inside amethyst geodes.
tooltip.starcatcher.whiteveil.0: A pale long-finned fish common underground.
tooltip.starcatcher.whiteveil.1: surprisingly heavy for what looks like a ghost.
tooltip.starcatcher.black_eel.0: A carnivorous black eel common underground.
tooltip.starcatcher.amethystback.0: A rare hard to catch fish common close to amethyst geodes.
tooltip.starcatcher.stonefish.0: A huge heavy fish made of stone,
tooltip.starcatcher.fossilized_angelfish: It is not actually fossilised and is often
ignored due to its low nutritional value,
heavy weight and high difficulty to catch.
tooltip.starcatcher.dripfin.0: A basic fish which inhabits the
tooltip.starcatcher.yellowstone_fish.0: A beautiful gold colored fish common to dripstone caves.
tooltip.starcatcher.lush_pike.1: found in the Lush Caves. A real tough
tooltip.starcatcher.vivid_moss.0: A piece of moss resembling a fish found in Lush Caves.
tooltip.starcatcher.vivid_moss.1: Or perhaps an actual fish consumed in moss?
tooltip.starcatcher.vivid_moss.2: Either way it's a hard catch due to its slipperiness.
tooltip.starcatcher.the_quarrish.0: A fish often seen around the overgrown lush caves.
tooltip.starcatcher.the_quarrish.1: Its heavy size makes for a very tedious catch.
tooltip.starcatcher.ghostly_pike.0: A ghost-looking pike rarely found deep underground.
tooltip.starcatcher.aquamarine_pike.0: A predatory fish found deep underground.
tooltip.starcatcher.garnet_mackerel.0: A hard to catch mackerel species found deep in caves.
tooltip.starcatcher.bright_amethyst_snap: A gorgeous pink colored fish found at deepslate level.
It's said to always swim around with it's pair of Dark Amethyst Snapper.
tooltip.starcatcher.dark_amethyst_snappe: A gorgeous dark blue colored fish found at deepslate level.
It's said to always swim around with it's pair of Bright Amethyst Snapper.
tooltip.starcatcher.deepslatefish.0: A hard to catch fish found high up in the mountains.
tooltip.starcatcher.deepslatefish.1: Just kidding. It's found at deepslate.
tooltip.starcatcher.sculkfish.0: A tricky fish corrupted by Sculk.
tooltip.starcatcher.ward.0: A <sclegendary>legendary</sclegendary> fish said to be best friends with the warden.
tooltip.starcatcher.ward.1: Instead of resisting Sculk, this fish embraced it and became stronger for it.
tooltip.starcatcher.glowing_dark.0: A fast fish only found in the deep dark.
tooltip.starcatcher.suneater.0: A rare fish found in the lava lakes of the Overworld.
tooltip.starcatcher.suneater.1: It hunts down sunfishes for food.
tooltip.starcatcher.pyrotrout.0: A trout adapted to live in the surface lava of the Overworld.
tooltip.starcatcher.obsidian_eel.0: A <sclegendary>legendary</sclegendary> species of eel made of obsidian, found
tooltip.starcatcher.obsidian_eel.1: during rainy days inside Overworld lava lakes.
tooltip.starcatcher.obsidian_eel.2: It often requires several anglers to carry
tooltip.starcatcher.molten_shrimp.0: A small shrimp found in lava rivers underground.
tooltip.starcatcher.obsidian_crab.0: An illusive crab found close to underground lava pools.
tooltip.starcatcher.scorched_bloodsucker: A hard to catch parasite found in deepslate lava lakes.
tooltip.starcatcher.molten_deepslate_cra: A gold colored crab found at deepslate levels close to lava rivers.
tooltip.starcatcher.cerberay.1: The 2 smaller ones only share 1 brain
tooltip.starcatcher.embergill.0: A fish found in the vast lava oceans of The Nether.
tooltip.starcatcher.scalding_pike.0: A predatory fish only found in The Nether.
tooltip.starcatcher.cinder_squid.0: A slippery squid found in The Nether.
tooltip.starcatcher.lava_crab.0: This particular species of crab somehow entered The Nether
tooltip.starcatcher.lava_crab.1: and evolved a protective shell out of netherrack.
tooltip.starcatcher.magma_fish.0: A common Nether fish known for it's red glowing fins.
tooltip.starcatcher.glowstone_seeker.0: A fish which seems to be weirdly attracted
tooltip.starcatcher.glowstone_seeker.1: to glowstone, usually swimming under it.
tooltip.starcatcher.glowstone_pufferfish: A pufferfish glittering just like glowstone.
tooltip.starcatcher.willish.0: A fish known for scamming other fishes out of their food.
tooltip.starcatcher.willish.1: It is incredibly ugly and should be discarded back to The Nether where it belongs.
tooltip.starcatcher.lava_crab_claw.0: A claw ripped off from a Lava Crab.
tooltip.starcatcher.lava_crab_claw.1: Due to the aggressive nature of Lava Crabs,
tooltip.starcatcher.lava_crab_claw.2: it's quite a common find in The Nether.
tooltip.starcatcher.charfish.0: A purple fish only found in The End.
tooltip.starcatcher.chorus_crab.0: This crab species uses Chorus fruit as it's main source of food.
tooltip.starcatcher.chorus_crab.1: It is said it can teleport around at will after feeding.
tooltip.starcatcher.end_glow.0: A tricky fish only found in The End.
tooltip.starcatcher.end_glow.1: Enderman don't seem to mind it.
tooltip.starcatcher.voidbiter.0: A <sclegendary>legendary</sclegendary> fish found floating in the vast void of The End.
tooltip.starcatcher.voidbiter.1: So few anglers have seen it that it's only rumored to exist.
tooltip.starcatcher.voidbiter.2: It's said only <scuncommon>Older Anglers</scuncommon> have successfully
tooltip.starcatcher.purple_carp.0: These species of carp have adapted to the cold void of The End.
tooltip.starcatcher.purple_carp.1: It is said to make a great stew,
tooltip.starcatcher.purple_carp.2: although no one is sure how they cook it.
tooltip.starcatcher.voidfin.0: A pale purple fish known for its large fins used to glide the void.
tooltip.starcatcher.spacejelly.1: It floats around the outer islands of The End in search of End Cities.
tooltip.starcatcher.chorus_minnow.0: A species of Minnor consumed by Chorus.
tooltip.starcatcher.chorus_minnow.1: It is unknown if they are native to The End.
tooltip.starcatcher.nebula_squid.0: Unlike the Cinder Squid, Nebula Squids are quite sticky,
tooltip.starcatcher.nebula_squid.1: and often use their tentacles to wrap around chorus fruits whilst feeding.
tooltip.starcatcher.coggill.0: A dusty construct made of brass gears and cogs often used in arid areas to move air around.
tooltip.starcatcher.mechanical_brass_sna: A machine made to clean oil off of beaches.
This one seems to have been upgraded to brass, making it more efficient.
tooltip.starcatcher.mechanical_snail.0: A machine made to clean oil off of beaches.
tooltip.starcatcher.mechanical_snail.1: It slowly moves back and forth cleaning up polution,
tooltip.starcatcher.mechanical_snail.2: left behind by whoever made it.
tooltip.starcatcher.phillipsfish.0: This machine drifts around the ocean and plugs itself wherever it can fit in.
tooltip.starcatcher.valve.0: A loose valve attached to a pipe.
tooltip.starcatcher.valve.1: Somewhere, someone is wondering where it went.
tooltip.starcatcher.pipehead.0: This type of machine, sometimes called a pipesucker,
tooltip.starcatcher.pipehead.1: is often used to connect different mechanisms underwater.
tooltip.starcatcher.pipehead.2: It would appear this one got loose and started drifting the oceans.
tooltip.starcatcher.cogtopus.0: A very intelligent machine made to mimic the behaviour of octopus.
tooltip.starcatcher.cogtopus.1: Each of its brains (cogs) controls a different arm, making it able to maneuver anywhere it pleases.
tooltip.starcatcher.eel_dynamo.0: A oily slippery eel ran by gears.
tooltip.starcatcher.drive_pike.0: An aggressive machine made to control the fish population around rivers
tooltip.starcatcher.brassgill.0: A mechanical construct resembling the Embergill,
tooltip.starcatcher.brassgill.1: made to survive high temperature fluids.
tooltip.starcatcher.meka_agave_bream.0: The mechanical version of an Agave Bream made for controlling bream population.
tooltip.starcatcher.meka_agave_bream.1: If it's unable to find a suitable Agave Bream in time it will hibernate until next mating season.
